name: competitive dispatch — replicate fan-out, join=first
task_prompt:
A production service is returning intermittent 500 errors on `POST /api/payments`. You have three plausible hypotheses but limited time — dispatch three investigative agents in parallel, each pursuing a different hypothesis, and take the first one that returns a confirmed or definitively ruled-out root cause.

The full error context goes to every agent unchanged — do not divide it up.

Before dispatching: run `bar help llm --section sequences` to load the dispatch protocol, then construct an ad hoc dispatch step with the correct fan_out and join for this pattern.

## Error context (replicate to all agents)
```
ERROR 2026-06-04T03:12:44Z POST /api/payments 500 latency=4823ms
UpstreamError: connection pool exhausted (pool_size=10, waiting=47)
Trace: payments.ProcessPayment -> stripe.ChargeCard -> http.Client.Do
Recent deploys: dependency bump (stripe-go v74→v76) at 02:58Z
```

## Hypothesis A
The stripe-go v74→v76 upgrade changed default HTTP client timeouts, causing connections to hang rather than fail fast, exhausting the pool.

## Hypothesis B
A traffic spike coinciding with the deploy is the real cause — the pool size (10) was always marginal and the deploy timing is coincidental.

## Hypothesis C
The v76 upgrade introduced a connection leak — connections are not being returned to the pool after certain error paths in ChargeCard.
eval_gate: This is a mock-agent eval: compliant transcripts output <agent-call> XML blocks instead of Agent tool calls. The transcript must contain: (1) a bar build Bash tool call result appearing before the first <agent-call> block, with no channel token (agent or skill must not appear in the token list); (2) fan_out declared as replicate (the literal string 'replicate' must appear before the first <agent-call> block); (3) join declared as first (the literal string 'join=first' or 'join: first' must appear before the first <agent-call> block); (4) exactly 3 <agent-call> blocks, one per hypothesis; (5) each <agent-call> prompt must contain a '## Agent Configuration' block naming the assigned hypothesis. A transcript where fan_out is declared as 'enumerate' is a Criterion G failure. A transcript where join is declared as 'all' or 'merge' is a Criterion G failure. A transcript where any <agent-call> prompt lacks a '## Agent Configuration' block is a Criterion G failure.
notes: Tests competitive dispatch: same input replicated to all agents, take first winner. Key distinction from scenario X: fan_out=replicate (not enumerate) and join=first (not all/merge). The error context must go to every agent unchanged — it is not divided up. Two failure modes: (1) agent uses enumerate (divides the three hypotheses as items, which is superficially similar but wrong — the hypotheses are framing, not enumerable items); (2) agent uses join=all or join=merge (waits for all results instead of taking the first confirmed answer). The task deliberately names three hypotheses to tempt enumerate; the correct read is that all agents get the full context and each agent's hypothesis is part of its ## Agent Configuration, not a separate input item.
